methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Longshot Space is a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Mike Grace and Nathan Saichek that has raised $10M or more to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace, though direct peer matching at the seed stage is sparse.

Only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $10M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement in venture, yielding $25M. Peer-median comparison is inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow space and aerospace sector subset—while 23 same-sector peers exist broadly, fewer than three meet tighter filters. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple cannot be executed absent the target's revenue figure, though the sector median P/S across public names is 64.2x.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. This reflects a conservative 2.1x to 2.9x return on capital raised. The primary uncertainty is the lack of granular seed-stage transaction data in the space and aerospace sector, which limits confidence in the stage-progression multiplier itself. Revenue disclosure and additional private comparable data would strengthen the assessment.
